title: batrachium baudotii subsp. leontinensis
intro: Batrachium baudotii subsp. leontinensis, also known as the aquatic liverwort, is a type of non-vascular plant that belongs to the family Jungermanniaceae. Traditionally used in folk medicine, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Batrachium baudotii subsp. leontinensis are said to include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested, dried, and then used to create infusions, teas, or decoctions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
